title: Java Stream API - Sorting elements
question: How to use Stream.sorted() in Java?
answer:
The sorted() method orders stream elements either naturally or by a custom comparator.

**Syntax:**
```java
Stream<T> sorted()  // Natural order
Stream<T> sorted(Comparator<? super T> comparator)  // Custom order
```

**Example:**
```java
List<Person> persons = Arrays.asList(
    new Person("p1", 1000),
    new Person("p2", 10000),
    new Person("p3", 9000)
);

// Sort by salary (ascending)
List<Person> sortedBySalary = persons.stream()
    .sorted((a, b) -> a.getSalary() - b.getSalary())
    .limit(2)
    .collect(Collectors.toList());

// Sort by salary (descending)
List<Person> topEarners = persons.stream()
    .sorted(Comparator.comparing(Person::getSalary).reversed())
    .collect(Collectors.toList());
```

**Use cases:**
- Ordering collections
- Finding top/bottom N elements
- Custom sorting logic
- Sorting by multiple fields
